source.title: Hippocampus as comparator: role of the two input and two output systems of the hippocampus in selection and registration of information

confidence_rationale: Foundational comparator framework. CA3 computes match/mismatch between two functionally distinct input streams (brainstem-reticular vs cortical) and gates both intra-hippocampal flow and subcortical arousal. Direct support for an anchor-side comparator-derived component to MECH-287 distinct from the LC/DA broadcast trigger. Supports per-stream V_s as biologically motivated rather than ad-hoc.
failure_signatures: If MECH-287 registers only the LC/DA broadcast trigger and omits a CA1/CA3 comparator component, it loses the local mismatch-driven gating documented here.

Two-stream architecture in original work is broad-strokes; modern literature recognises more substreams (MEC vs LEC, CA3 vs DG). The substrate plan's five-stream V_s is more granular than the comparator literature directly supports.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hippocampal CA3 receives two functionally distinct input streams (brainstem-reticular via MS-DB; cortical via EC through DG) and computes a match/mismatch comparison between them. The mismatch signal gates intra-hippocampal output (CA3 -> CA1) and subcortical arousal modulation (CA3 -> raphe -> reticular formation).
mapping.ree_translation: Anchor-side mismatch computation (per-anchor V_s in substrate plan) has direct biological precedent in the CA3 comparator function. MECH-287 broadcast trigger should consider registering an additional anchor-side comparator-derived component anatomically distinct from the LC/DA broadcast trigger documented in the waking-V_s lit-pull. Per-stream V_s extends the two-stream comparator principle to a higher-granularity per-stream mismatch computation.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Decades-old synthesis primarily from rabbit unit recordings; specific anatomical claims need updating. Five-stream V_s is more granular than the two-stream original literature directly supports.
mapping.source_context: In vivo unit recording synthesis in unanesthetised rabbits, with electrical stimulation and disconnection paradigms.

confidence_components.notes: Foundational paper; mapping fidelity moderate-to-high for the comparator architecture. Transfer risk moderate due to age of original work and rabbit-only data.
